top_categories.workout_context: 6,007ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Chile's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
79 miles on brutal paved rollers with wine valley stunners burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
76ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Chile. Train it until it feels boring.
GFNY Chile's brutal paved rollers with wine valley stunners forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 79 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,007ft of climbing on brutal paved rollers with wine valley stunners does to your power file on race day.
Base fitness for GFNY Chile. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
At GFNY Chile, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on brutal paved rollers with wine valley stunners. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
76–85% FTP for extended efforts. At GFNY Chile, tempo is the floor you'll live on. Make it feel easy.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for GFNY Chile’s 79 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at GFNY Chile. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 79 miles of brutal paved rollers with wine valley stunners in Casablanca, Valparaíso Region, Chile.
